code: assoc_subgroup(   data,   outcome_col,   time_col = NULL,   exposure_col,   by,   method = c("coxph", "logistic", "linear"),   covariates = NULL,   interaction = TRUE,   conf_level = 0.95 )  assoc_sub(   data,   outcome_col,   time_col = NULL,   exposure_col,   by,   method = c("coxph", "logistic", "linear"),   covariates = NULL,   interaction = TRUE,   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: Subgroup association analysis with optional interaction test — assoc_subgroup
text: data (data.frame data.table) Analysis dataset. outcome_col (character) Outcome column name. time_col (character NULL) Follow-time column (required method = "coxph"). exposure_col (character) One exposure variable names. (character) Single stratification variable name. unique non-NA values (factor levels, order) define subgroups. categorical binary variable small number levels (e.g. sex, smoking status). Continuous variables technically permitted interaction LRT still run, per-unique-value subgrouping rarely meaningful practice — use pre-categorised version (e.g. via derive_cut) instead. method (character) Regression method: "coxph" (default), "logistic", "linear". covariates (character NULL) Covariate column names Fully adjusted model. NULL, Unadjusted model run. interaction (logical) Compute LRT p-value exposure \(\times\) interaction full dataset. Default: TRUE. conf_level (numeric) Confidence level. Default: 0.95.

text: data.table one row per subgroup level \(\times\) exposure \(\times\) term \(\times\) model, containing: subgroup Name variable. subgroup_level Factor: level variable (ordered original level / sort order). exposure Exposure variable name. term Coefficient name fitted model. model Ordered factor: Unadjusted < Fully adjusted. n Participants model (NA removal). ... Effect estimate columns: HR//beta, CI_lower, CI_upper, p_value, formatted label column. Cox models additionally include n_events person_years; logistic models include n_cases; linear models include se. p_interaction LRT p-value exposure \(\times\) interaction full dataset. Shared across subgroup levels exposure \(\times\) model. NA interaction model fails. present interaction = TRUE.

title: Subgroup association analysis with optional interaction test — assoc_subgroup
text: Unadjusted - always run (covariates). Fully adjusted - run covariates non-NULL. Users responsible excluding variable covariates (warning issued included). Interaction test: interaction = TRUE (default), likelihood ratio test (LRT) exposure \(\times\) interaction computed full dataset exposure \(\times\) model combination appended p_interaction. LRT preferred Wald handles factor, binary, continuous variables uniformly without requiring user recode variable.

title: Dose-response trend analysis — assoc_trend
text: data (data.frame data.table) Analysis dataset. outcome_col (character) Outcome column name. time_col (character NULL) Follow-time column (required method = "coxph"). exposure_col (character) One exposure column names. must factor (ordered unordered). first level used reference group. method (character) Regression method: "coxph" (default), "logistic", "linear". covariates (character NULL) Covariates Fully adjusted model. Default: NULL. base (logical) Include Unadjusted Age--sex-adjusted models. Default: TRUE. scores (numeric NULL) Numeric scores assigned factor levels level order. Length must equal nlevels every exposure. NULL (default) uses 0, 1, 2, ... conf_level (numeric) Confidence level. Default: 0.95.

title: Dose-response trend analysis — assoc_trend
text: data.table one row per exposure \(\times\) level \(\times\) model, containing: exposure Exposure variable name. level Factor level (ordered factor preserving original level order). term Coefficient name returned model (reference row uses paste0(exposure, ref_level)). model Ordered factor: Unadjusted < Age sex adjusted < Fully adjusted. n Participants model (NA removal). ... Effect estimate columns categorical model: HR//beta, CI_lower, CI_upper, p_value, formatted label. Reference row HR = 1 / beta = 0 NA CI p. Cox models additionally include n_events person_years; logistic includes n_cases; linear includes se. HR_per_score / OR_per_score / beta_per_score Per-score-unit effect estimate trend model. Shared across levels within exposure \(\times\) model. HR_per_score_label / OR_per_score_label / beta_per_score_label Formatted string per-score estimate. p_trend P-value linear trend trend model. Shared across levels within exposure \(\times\) model.

title: Dose-response trend analysis — assoc_trend
text: Categorical model - exposure treated factor; produces one row per non-reference level (HR / / \(\beta\) vs reference). Trend model - exposure recoded numeric scores (default: 0, 1, 2, ...); produces per-score-unit effect estimate (*_per_score) p_trend. results merged: output contains reference row (effect = 1 / 0, CI = NA) followed non-reference rows, *_per_score p_trend appended shared columns (value within exposure \(\times\) model combination). Scores: default levels scored 0, 1, 2, ... reference group = 0 step = 1 unit. Supply scores use meaningful units (e.g. median years per category) - p_trend per-score estimate change; per-category HRs unaffected. Adjustment models: follows logic assoc_coxph - Unadjusted Age--sex-adjusted models included default (base = TRUE); Fully adjusted model added covariates non-NULL.

title: Dose-response trend analysis — assoc_trend
code: dt <- ops_toy(scenario = "association", n = 500) #> ✔ ops_toy: 500 participants | 33 columns | scenario = "association" | seed = 42 dt <- dt[dm_timing != 1L]  res <- assoc_trend(   data         = dt,   outcome_col  = "dm_status",   time_col     = "dm_followup_years",   exposure_col = "bmi_cat",   method       = "coxph",   covariates   = c("tdi_cat", "p20116_i0"),   base         = FALSE ) #> ! Exposure bmi_cat is not an ordered factor -- levels will be scored 0, 1, 2, ... (equal spacing assumed). #> ℹ outcome_col dm_status: logical detected, converting TRUE/FALSE -> 1/0 #>  #> ── assoc_trend ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ 1 exposure x 1 model (categorical + trend model per combination) #>  #> ── bmi_cat ── #>  #> ℹ Levels: Underweight -> Normal -> Overweight -> Obese | Scores: 0, 1, 2, 3 #> ℹ   Fully adjusted | bmi_catUnderweight: 1.00 (ref) #> ✔   Fully adjusted | bmi_catNormal: HR 0.40 (0.10-1.64), p = 0.203 #> ✔   Fully adjusted | bmi_catOverweight: HR 0.40 (0.10-1.61), p = 0.196 #> ✔   Fully adjusted | bmi_catObese: HR 0.64 (0.17-2.43), p = 0.516 #> ℹ   Fully adjusted | trend: HR_per_score = 1.03 (0.66-1.60), p_trend = 0.893 #> ✔ Done: 4 result rows across 1 exposure and 1 model.

title: Compute age at event for one or more UKB outcomes — derive_age
text: name name, adds one column age_at_{name} (numeric, years) computed : $$age\_at\_event = age\_col + (event\_date - baseline\_date) / 365.25$$

title: Compute age at event for one or more UKB outcomes — derive_age
code: derive_age(   data,   name,   baseline_col,   age_col,   date_cols = NULL,   status_cols = NULL )

title: Compute age at event for one or more UKB outcomes — derive_age
text: data (data.frame data.table) UKB phenotype data. name (character) One output prefixes, e.g. c("disease", "disease_icd10", "outcome"). produces age_at_{name}. baseline_col (character) Name baseline date column (e.g. "date_baseline"). age_col (character) Name age--baseline column (e.g. "age_recruitment"). date_cols (character NULL) Named character vector mapping name event date column, e.g. c(disease = "disease_date", outcome = "outcome_date"). NULL (default) triggers auto-detection {name}_date. status_cols (character NULL) Named character vector mapping name status column. NULL (default) triggers auto-detection.

title: Compute age at event for one or more UKB outcomes — derive_age
text: input data one new age_at_{name} column per entry name, added -place.

title: Compute age at event for one or more UKB outcomes — derive_age
text: value NA participants experience event (status FALSE / 0) lack event date. Auto-detection per name (date_cols / status_cols NULL): date_col   - looked {name}_date. status_col - looked first {name}_status, {name} (logical column); neither exists rows non-NA date treated cases. data.table pass--reference: new columns added -place.

what: Derive a binary disease flag from UKB cancer registry — derive_cancer_registry
title: Derive a binary disease flag from UKB cancer registry — derive_cancer_registry
text: UK Biobank cancer registry links participant's cancer diagnoses national cancer registries.  diagnosis stored separate instance four parallel fields: ICD-10 code (p40006), histology code (p40011), behaviour code (p40012), diagnosis date (p40005).  Unlike HES self-report data, instance holds exactly one record - array (*) dimension.

title: Derive a binary disease flag from UKB cancer registry — derive_cancer_registry
code: derive_cancer_registry(   data,   name,   icd10 = NULL,   histology = NULL,   behaviour = NULL,   code_cols = NULL,   hist_cols = NULL,   behv_cols = NULL,   date_cols = NULL )

title: Derive a binary disease flag from UKB cancer registry — derive_cancer_registry
text: data (data.frame data.table) UKB phenotype data containing cancer registry fields. name (character) Output column prefix, e.g. "outcome_invasive" produces outcome_invasive_cancer outcome_invasive_cancer_date. icd10 (character NULL) Regular expression matched ICD-10 code column (p40006).  NULL = ICD-10 filter. Examples: "^C44", "^(C44|D04)". histology (integer vector NULL) Histology codes retain (p40011).  NULL = histology filter. Example: c(8070:8078, 8083, 8084). behaviour (integer vector NULL) Behaviour codes retain (p40012).  NULL = behaviour filter. Typical values: 3L (invasive / malignant), 2L (situ). code_cols (character NULL) Names ICD-10 code columns (p40006_i*).  NULL = auto-detect via field 40006. hist_cols (character NULL) Names histology columns (p40011_i*).  NULL = auto-detect via field 40011. behv_cols (character NULL) Names behaviour columns (p40012_i*).  NULL = auto-detect via field 40012. date_cols (character NULL) Names diagnosis date columns (p40005_i*).  NULL = auto-detect via field 40005.

title: Derive a binary disease flag from UKB cancer registry — derive_cancer_registry
text: input data two new columns added -place: {name}_cancer (logical) {name}_cancer_date (IDate). Always returns data.table.

title: Derive a binary disease flag from UKB cancer registry — derive_cancer_registry
text: three filter arguments (icd10, histology, behaviour) applied logic: record must satisfy every non-NULL filter counted.  conditions (e.g.\ D04 C44 specific histology), call function twice combine resulting columns downstream. {name}_cancer Logical flag: TRUE cancer registry record satisfies supplied filters. {name}_cancer_date Earliest matching diagnosis date (IDate).

title: Derive a binary disease flag from UKB cancer registry — derive_cancer_registry

what: Prepare UKB covariates for analysis — derive_covariate
title: Prepare UKB covariates for analysis — derive_covariate
text: Converts decoded UKB columns analysis-ready types: character-encoded numeric fields numeric, categorical fields factor. Prints concise summary converted column - mean / median / SD / missing rate numeric columns, level counts factor columns - can verify distributions without leaving pipeline.

title: Prepare UKB covariates for analysis — derive_covariate
code: derive_covariate(   data,   as_numeric = NULL,   as_factor = NULL,   factor_levels = NULL,   max_levels = 5L )

title: Prepare UKB covariates for analysis — derive_covariate
text: data (data.frame data.table) UKB data, typically output derive_missing. as_numeric (character NULL) Column names convert numeric. Values coerced (e.g. residual text) become NA warning. Default: NULL. as_factor (character NULL) Column names convert factor. Default levels sorted unique non-NA values unless overridden factor_levels. Default: NULL. factor_levels (named list NULL) Custom level ordering specific factor columns. Names must match entries as_factor; values character vectors levels desired order (first level = reference group regression). Columns listed use default ordering. Default: NULL. max_levels (integer) Factor columns levels threshold trigger warning suggesting user consider collapsing categories. Default: 5L.

title: Prepare UKB covariates for analysis — derive_covariate
text: input data converted columns. Always returns data.table.

text: data.table pass--reference: input data.table, modifications made -place. Pass data.table::copy(data) preserve original.

title: Cut a continuous UKB variable into quantile-based or custom groups — derive_cut
text: data (data.frame data.table) UKB data. col (character) Name source numeric column. n (integer) Number groups. integer >= 2 accepted. Commonly used values 2, 3, 4, 5. breaks (numeric vector NULL) Interior cut points; length must equal n - 1. NULL (default), quantile-based equal- frequency boundaries computed automatically. labels (character vector NULL) Group labels length n. Defaults "Q1", "Q2", ..., "Qn". name (character NULL) Name new column. Defaults "{col}_bi" / "{col}_tri" / "{col}_quad" / "{col}_quin" n = 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; values n produce "{col}_g{n}".

Pass data.table::copy(data) preserve original.

title: Convert UKB imputed BGEN files to PGEN on RAP — grs_bgen2pgen
text: chr Integer vector. Chromosomes process. Default: 1:22. dest Character scalar. RAP destination path output PGEN files (e.g. "/pgen/"). Must specified explicitly. maf Numeric scalar. Minor allele frequency filter passed plink2 --maf. Variants MAF threshold excluded. Default: 0.01. Must (0, 0.5). instance Character scalar. Instance type preset: "standard" "large". See Details. Default: "standard". priority Character scalar. Job priority: "low" "high". Default: "low".

title: Convert UKB imputed BGEN files to PGEN on RAP — grs_bgen2pgen
text: character vector job IDs (one per chromosome), returned invisibly. Failed submissions NA. Use job_ls, job_status, job_wait monitor progress.

title: Convert UKB imputed BGEN files to PGEN on RAP — grs_bgen2pgen
text: function auto-generates plink2 driver script, uploads RAP project root (/) RAP, loops chr submitting one job per chromosome. 500 ms pause submissions prevents API rate-limiting. Output path critical. driver script writes plink2 output /home/dnanexus/// - fixed path Swiss Army Knife auto-uploads dest completion. Output files per chromosome: chr{N}_maf001.pgen/.pvar/.psam/.log. Instance types: "standard" mem2_ssd1_v2_x4: 4 cores, 12 GB RAM. Suitable smaller chromosomes (roughly chr 17–22). "large" mem2_ssd2_v2_x8: 8 cores, 28 GB RAM, 640 GB SSD. Required large chromosomes (roughly chr 1–16) standard storage insufficient.

title: Convert UKB imputed BGEN files to PGEN on RAP — grs_bgen2pgen

what: Wait for a DNAnexus job to finish — job_wait
title: Wait for a DNAnexus job to finish — job_wait

title: Wait for a DNAnexus job to finish — job_wait
code: job_wait(job_id, interval = 30, timeout = Inf, verbose = TRUE)

title: Wait for a DNAnexus job to finish — job_wait
text: job_id (character) Job ID returned extract_batch(). interval (integer) Polling interval seconds. Default: 30. timeout (numeric) Maximum wait time seconds. Default: Inf (wait indefinitely). UKB RAP, jobs can stay "runnable" several hours peak times — set finite value (e.g. 7200) need hard deadline. verbose (logical) Print state elapsed time poll. Default: TRUE.

text: Invisibly returns final state string ("done").

what: Record and review dataset pipeline snapshots — ops_snapshot
title: Record and review dataset pipeline snapshots — ops_snapshot

title: Record and review dataset pipeline snapshots — ops_snapshot

Defaults "snapshot_N" N sequential index. reset (logical) TRUE, clears entire snapshot history returns invisibly. Default FALSE. verbose (logical) Print CLI report. Default TRUE. check_na (logical) Whether count columns NA blank string values include delta report. Set FALSE skip NA scan (useful large datasets NA tracking needed). Default TRUE.

title: Record and review dataset pipeline snapshots — ops_snapshot

what: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
text: Creates small, synthetic dataset mimics structure UK Biobank phenotype data RAP. Useful developing testing derive_*, assoc_*, plot_* functions without requiring real UKB data access.

title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
code: ops_toy(scenario = "cohort", n = 1000L, seed = 42L)

title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
text: scenario (character) Data structure generate: "cohort": wide participant-level table raw UKB field columns full derive_* → assoc_* → plot_* pipeline. "association": analysis-ready table covariates already factors, BMI/TDI binned, two pre-derived disease outcomes (dm_*, htn_*) including status, date, timing, follow-columns. Use assoc_* examples testing without running derive pipeline. "forest": association results table matching assoc_coxph() output, testing plot_forest(). n = number exposures (default 8). n (integer) Number participants (exposures "forest"). Default 1000L "cohort", 2000L "association", 8L "forest". seed (integer NULL) Random seed reproducibility. Pass NULL different dataset every call. Default 42L.

title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
text: data.table UKB-style column names. See Details columns included scenario.

title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
text: dataset entirely synthetic. Column names follow RAP conventions (e.g. p41270, p20002_i0_a0).

title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
text: Includes following column groups: Demographics: eid, p31, p34, p53_i0, p21022 Covariates: p21001_i0, p20116_i0, p1558_i0, p21000_i0, p22189, p54_i0 Genetic PCs: p22009_a1 – p22009_a10 Self-report disease: p20002_i0_a0–a4, p20008_i0_a0–a4 Self-report cancer: p20001_i0_a0–a4, p20006_i0_a0–a4 HES: p41270 (JSON array), p41280_a0–a8 Cancer registry: p40006_i0–i2, p40011_i0–i2, p40012_i0–i2, p40005_i0–i2 Death registry: p40001_i0, p40002_i0_a0–a2, p40000_i0 First occurrence: p131742 GRS: grs_bmi, grs_raw, grs_finngen Messy columns: messy_allna, messy_empty, messy_label

title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
text: Analysis-ready table. derive inputs (raw arrays, HES JSON, registry fields) omitted; derive outputs pre-computed internally consistent relationships: Demographics: eid, p31 (factor), p53_i0 (IDate), p21022 Covariates: p21001_i0, bmi_cat (factor, derived p21001_i0), p20116_i0 (factor), p1558_i0 (factor), p21000_i0 (factor), p22189, tdi_cat (factor, derived p22189 quartiles), p54_i0 (factor) Genetic PCs: p22009_a1 – p22009_a10 GRS: grs_bmi (continuous exposure) DM outcome: dm_status, dm_date, dm_timing, dm_followup_end, dm_followup_years (type 2 diabetes, ~12% prevalence) HTN outcome: htn_status, htn_date, htn_timing, htn_followup_end, htn_followup_years (hypertension, ~28% prevalence) Internal relationships guaranteed: bmi_cat cut p21001_i0 (breaks 18.5 / 25 / 30) tdi_cat cut p22189 quartiles dm_date NA iff dm_status = FALSE dm_timing: 0 = disease, 1 = prevalent, 2 = incident, NA = date dm_followup_years NA prevalent cases (dm_timing == 1)

title: Generate toy UKB-like data for testing and development — ops_toy
code: # cohort: raw UKB-style columns, feed into derive pipeline dt <- ops_toy(n = 100) #> ✔ ops_toy: 100 participants | 75 columns | scenario = "cohort" | seed = 42 dt <- derive_missing(dt) #> ✔ derive_missing: replaced 47 values across 3 columns (action = "na").  # association: analysis-ready, feed directly into assoc_* functions dt <- ops_toy(scenario = "association", n = 500) #> ✔ ops_toy: 500 participants | 33 columns | scenario = "association" | seed = 42 dt <- dt[dm_timing != 1L]   # exclude prevalent cases  # forest: results table for plot_forest() dt <- ops_toy(scenario = "forest") #> ✔ ops_toy: 24 rows | 11 columns | scenario = "forest" | seed = 42

title: Publication-ready forest plot — plot_forest
text: Produces publication-ready forest plot UKB-standard styling. user supplies data frame whose first column row label (item), plus additional display columns (e.g. Cases/N). gap column auto-formatted (95% CI) text column inserted automatically ci_column. Numeric p-value columns declared via p_cols formatted -place.

title: Publication-ready forest plot — plot_forest
code: plot_forest(   data,   est,   lower,   upper,   ci_column = 2L,   ref_line = 1,   xlim = NULL,   ticks_at = NULL,   arrow_lab = c("Lower risk", "Higher risk"),   header = NULL,   indent = NULL,   bold_label = NULL,   ci_col = "black",   ci_sizes = 0.6,   ci_Theight = 0.2,   ci_digits = 2L,   ci_sep = ", ",   p_cols = NULL,   p_digits = 3L,   bold_p = TRUE,   p_threshold = 0.05,   align = NULL,   background = "zebra",   bg_col = "#F0F0F0",   border = "three_line",   border_width = 3,   row_height = NULL,   col_width = NULL,   save = FALSE,   dest = NULL,   save_width = 20,   save_height = NULL,   theme = "default" )

title: Publication-ready forest plot — plot_forest
text: data data.frame. First column must label column (item). Additional columns displayed -(character) formatted named p_cols (must numeric). Column order preserved. est Numeric vector. Point estimates (NA = CI drawn). lower Numeric vector. Lower CI bounds (length est). upper Numeric vector. Upper CI bounds (length est). ci_column Integer. Column position final rendered table gap/CI graphic placed. Must 2 ncol(data) + 1 (inclusive). Default: 2L. ref_line Numeric. Reference line. Default: 1 (HR/). Use 0 beta coefficients. xlim Numeric vector length 2. X-axis limits. NULL (default) uses c(0, 2). ticks_at Numeric vector. Tick positions. NULL (default) = 5 evenly spaced ticks across xlim. arrow_lab Character vector length 2. Directional labels. Default: c("Lower risk", "Higher risk"). NULL = none. header Character vector length ncol(data) + 2. Column header labels final rendered table (original columns + gap_ci + label). NULL (default) = use column names data plus "gap_ci" "(95% CI)". Pass "" gap column position. indent Integer vector (length = nrow(data)). Indentation level label column: unit adds two leading spaces. Default: zeros. bold_label Logical vector (length = nrow(data)). rows bold label column. NULL (default) = auto-derive indent: rows indent == 0 bolded (parent rows), indented sub-rows plain. ci_col Character scalar vector (length = nrow(data)). CI colour(s). NA rows skipped automatically. Default: "black". ci_sizes Numeric. Point size. Default: 0.6. ci_Theight Numeric. CI cap height. Default: 0.2. ci_digits Integer. Decimal places auto-generated (95% CI) column. Default: 2L. ci_sep Character. Separator lower upper CI label, e.g. ", " " - ". Default: ", ". p_cols Character vector. Names numeric p-value columns data. formatted p_digits decimal places "<0.001"-style clipping. NULL = none. p_digits Integer. Decimal places p-value formatting. Default: 3L. bold_p TRUE (bold non-NA p p_threshold), FALSE (bolding), logical vector (per-row control). Default: TRUE. p_threshold Numeric. P-value threshold bolding bold_p = TRUE. Default: 0.05. align Integer vector length ncol(data) + 2. Alignment per column: -1 left, 0 centre, 1 right. Must cover final columns (original + gap_ci + label). NULL = auto (column 1 left, others centre). background Character. Row background style: "zebra", "bold_label" (shade rows bold_label = TRUE), "none". Default: "zebra". bg_col Character. Shading colour backgrounds (scalar), per-row vector length nrow(data) (overrides style). Default: "#F0F0F0". border Character. Border style: "three_line" "none". Default: "three_line". border_width Numeric. Border line width(s). Scalar = three lines width; length-3 vector = top--header, bottom--header, bottom--body. Default: 3. row_height NULL (auto), numeric scalar, numeric vector (length = total gtable rows including margins). Auto sets 8 / 12 / 10 / 15 mm top / header / data / bottom respectively. col_width NULL (auto), numeric scalar, numeric vector (length = total gtable columns). Auto rounds default width adjustment \([5, 10)\) mm. save Logical. Save output files? Default: FALSE. dest Character. Destination file path (extension ignored; four formats saved). Required save = TRUE. save_width Numeric. Output width cm. Default: 20. save_height Numeric NULL. Output height cm. NULL = nrow(data) * 0.9 + 3. theme Character preset ("default") forestploter::forest_theme object. Default: "default".

title: Publication-ready forest plot — plot_forest
text: forestploter plot object (gtable), returned invisibly. Display plot() grid::grid.draw().

title: Publication-ready forest plot — plot_forest
code: df <- data.frame(   item      = c("Exposure vs. control", "Unadjusted", "Fully adjusted"),   `Cases/N` = c("", "89/4521", "89/4521"),   p_value   = c(NA_real_, 0.001, 0.006),   check.names = FALSE )  p <- plot_forest(   data       = df,   est        = c(NA, 1.52, 1.43),   lower      = c(NA, 1.18, 1.11),   upper      = c(NA, 1.96, 1.85),   ci_column  = 2L,   indent     = c(0L, 1L, 1L),   bold_label = c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE),   p_cols     = "p_value",   xlim       = c(0.5, 3.0) ) plot(p)

title: Publication-ready Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) — plot_tableone
text: Generates publication-quality baseline-characteristics table (Table 1) using gtsummary, optional SMD column, Lancet-style theming, automatic export four formats.

title: Publication-ready Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) — plot_tableone
code: plot_tableone(   data,   vars,   strata = NULL,   type = NULL,   label = NULL,   statistic = NULL,   digits = NULL,   percent = "column",   missing = "no",   add_p = TRUE,   add_smd = FALSE,   overall = FALSE,   exclude_labels = NULL,   theme = "lancet",   label_width = 200,   stat_width = 140,   pvalue_width = 100,   row_height = 8,   save = FALSE,   dest = NULL,   png_scale = 2,   pdf_width = NULL,   pdf_height = NULL )

title: Publication-ready Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) — plot_tableone
text: data data.frame. Input data containing variables. vars Character vector. Variable names display table. strata Character scalar. Column name grouping/stratification variable. NULL produces unstratified overall summary. type Named list NULL. Variable type overrides passed directly tbl_summary. E.g. list(age = "continuous2"). label Named list / formula list NULL. Label overrides. E.g. list(age ~ "Age (years)", sex ~ "Sex"). statistic Named list NULL. Statistic format strings. E.g. list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})"). digits Named list NULL. Decimal place overrides. E.g. list(age ~ 1). percent Character. Percentage base categorical variables: "column" (default), "row", "cell". missing Character. Display missing counts: "" (default), "ifany", "always". add_p Logical. Add p-value column. Default TRUE; disabled warning strata NULL. add_smd Logical. Add standardised mean difference (SMD) column. Continuous variables use Cohen's d; categorical variables use root-mean-square deviation (RMSD) group proportions. SMD shown label rows. Default FALSE; disabled warning strata NULL. overall Logical. Add Overall column strata set. Default FALSE. exclude_labels Character vector NULL. Row labels remove rendered table, matched exactly label column gtsummary table body. E.g. c("Unknown", "Missing"). theme Character. Visual theme preset. "lancet" (default) currently supported. Controls alternating row shading (#f8e8e7 / white), three-line borders, 15 px font size. label_width Integer. Label column width pixels. Default 200. stat_width Integer. Statistics column(s) width pixels. Default 140. pvalue_width Integer. P-value SMD column width pixels. Default 100. row_height Integer. Data row padding (top + bottom) pixels. Default 8. save Logical. Export table files. Default FALSE. dest Character NULL. File path without extension. save = TRUE, four files written: <dest>.docx, <dest>.html, <dest>.pdf, <dest>.png. Required save = TRUE. png_scale Numeric. Zoom factor PNG export via webshot2. Higher values produce larger, higher-resolution images. Default: 2. pdf_width Numeric NULL. PDF paper width inches passed pagedown::chrome_print. larger value increases page size content fits single page. Default: NULL (Chrome default). pdf_height Numeric NULL. PDF paper height inches. Increase table cut across pages. Default: NULL.

title: Publication-ready Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) — plot_tableone
text: gt table object, returned invisibly.

title: Publication-ready Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) — plot_tableone
text: following behaviours fixed (exposed parameters): Variable labels bold (bold_labels()). P-values formatted <0.001 3 decimal places. Significant p-values (\(p < 0.05\)) bold. p-value column header rendered P-value. table uses three-line (booktabs) border. Saving always exports word, html, pdf, png.

what: ukbflow: Streamlined Workflow for UK Biobank Data Extraction, Analysis, and Visualization — ukbflow-package
title: ukbflow: Streamlined Workflow for UK Biobank Data Extraction, Analysis, and Visualization — ukbflow-package
text: Provides streamlined workflow UK Biobank cloud-based analysis Research Analysis Platform (RAP). Includes tools phenotype extraction decoding, variable derivation, survival association analysis, genetic risk score computation, publication-quality visualization. details UK Biobank resource, see Bycroft et al. (2018) doi:10.1038/s41586-018-0579-z .
